better_sources: Chrome Web Store review samples across recent time windows.
Support sites or help centers with reproducible complaint text.
GitHub issues only when the project is active and user-facing.
Reddit or forum corroboration for practitioner workflow pain.
category_strategy: Bias toward low-permission, local-only productivity wedges.
Do not build same-family variants unless differentiation is explicit and testable.
Allow no-go days when evidence stays weak.
keyword_strategy: Start from the user job, then add failure words such as skip, miss, overwrite, noisy, manual.
Prefer keywords that imply a narrow, testable happy path over broad category terms.
minimum_thresholds: At least one non-store source before repeated same-family builds
At least three meaningful pain clusters before build
negative_review_mining_strategy: Mine 1-star to 3-star reviews for repeated verbs such as skip, overwrite, fail, noisy, manual.
Separate workflow friction from field coverage and privacy complaints.
support_site_strategy: Prefer support pages with exact field or workflow failures.
Capture dated excerpts and URLs instead of paraphrasing.
github_issue_strategy: Use GitHub issues as corroboration when they include reproducible steps.
Down-rank stale trackers older than 180 days.
reddit_or_forum_strategy: Treat Reddit/forum evidence as corroboration, not the primary decision source.
Look for operator and recruiter communities where repetitive browser tasks are discussed.
recency_strategy: Bias toward evidence from the last 90 days.
Require newer corroboration when all evidence is older than 180 days.
anti_copycat_policy: Do not build just because a category is large.
Require a narrower user story or a lower-permission posture than existing tools.
portfolio_differentiation_strategy: Treat portfolio overlap as a real penalty, not just a warning.
When overlap is high, research more or skip instead of forcing a build.

next_10_search_queries.query: site:chromewebstore.google.com form filler one profile visible fields review
next_10_search_queries.hypothesis: Users still want a local-only one-profile form filler that avoids heavy dashboards.
next_10_search_queries.expected_user_pain: Missed fields, too many templates, overwrite anxiety.
next_10_search_queries.risk: High portfolio overlap if the wedge is still generic.
next_10_search_queries.why_now: Recent acceptance work exposed a sharper quality bar for form-fill wedges.
next_10_search_queries.exclude_if: Evidence stays store-only or pain remains vague.

next_10_search_queries.query: site:reddit.com recruiter intake form autofill local-only chrome extension
next_10_search_queries.hypothesis: Recruiter intake forms are specific enough to justify a narrower form-fill wedge.
next_10_search_queries.expected_user_pain: Repeated contact entry across similar fields.
next_10_search_queries.risk: Could still collapse into generic CRM autofill.
next_10_search_queries.why_now: The current form-fill archetype now has stronger test coverage.
next_10_search_queries.exclude_if: Support site and forum evidence disagree on the core flow.

next_10_search_queries.query: site:chromewebstore.google.com current window tab export csv pinned tabs review
next_10_search_queries.hypothesis: Current-window-only tab export remains a high-testability productivity wedge.
next_10_search_queries.expected_user_pain: All-session export is too broad and noisy.
next_10_search_queries.risk: Low differentiation if the wedge is just minor UI polish.
next_10_search_queries.why_now: This family remains low-permission and highly testable.
next_10_search_queries.exclude_if: User pain is mostly about advanced enterprise features.

next_10_search_queries.query: site:github.com browser tab export extension issue current window csv
next_10_search_queries.hypothesis: GitHub issue evidence can confirm whether export complaints are reproducible and recent.
next_10_search_queries.expected_user_pain: Noisy export columns and weak success feedback.
next_10_search_queries.risk: GitHub issues may reflect maintainer backlog instead of demand.
next_10_search_queries.why_now: Need stronger non-store corroboration before more same-family builds.
next_10_search_queries.exclude_if: Issue tracker is stale beyond 180 days.

next_10_search_queries.query: site:chromewebstore.google.com gmail snippet quick insert compose review
next_10_search_queries.hypothesis: Users want a two-click snippet insert, not a heavy side panel.
next_10_search_queries.expected_user_pain: Slow search and too much UI for a simple compose action.
next_10_search_queries.risk: Permission trust may dominate the complaints.
next_10_search_queries.why_now: Gmail snippets remain attractive only if the wedge stays lightweight.

next_10_search_queries.query: site:reddit.com canned reply chrome extension keyboard shortcut pain
next_10_search_queries.hypothesis: Forum users may reveal a sharper keyboard-first compose wedge.
next_10_search_queries.expected_user_pain: Templates feel slow when they interrupt the compose flow.
next_10_search_queries.risk: Forum anecdotes may be too thin without store corroboration.
next_10_search_queries.why_now: Need external evidence before greenlighting another compose helper.
next_10_search_queries.exclude_if: Pain cannot be converted into a deterministic happy path.

next_10_search_queries.query: site:chromewebstore.google.com small saas browser extension copy paste workflow review
next_10_search_queries.hypothesis: Operators still pay a tax on repetitive browser copy-paste flows.
next_10_search_queries.expected_user_pain: Manual cleanup, repeated entry, weak in-page feedback.
next_10_search_queries.risk: May be too broad unless the target workflow narrows fast.
next_10_search_queries.why_now: The factory should widen beyond the current candidate families without raising permission risk.
next_10_search_queries.exclude_if: The wedge implies multi-page automation or background sync.

next_10_search_queries.query: site:github.com browser extension one-click data cleanup issue
next_10_search_queries.hypothesis: Developer and operator utilities can yield narrow, local-only wedges.
next_10_search_queries.expected_user_pain: Too many cleanup steps before data is shareable.
next_10_search_queries.risk: Could drift into unsupported archetypes quickly.
next_10_search_queries.why_now: Need stronger low-permission alternatives to repeated form-fill work.
next_10_search_queries.exclude_if: The solution requires complex parsing across arbitrary sites.

next_10_search_queries.query: site:chromewebstore.google.com browser workflow friction local-only productivity review
next_10_search_queries.hypothesis: Some workflow wedges are attractive precisely because they stay local-only and shallow.
next_10_search_queries.expected_user_pain: Heavy dashboards for tasks that should be single-click.
next_10_search_queries.risk: May overlap with existing lightweight utilities.
next_10_search_queries.why_now: Discovery should bias toward low-permission, auditable problems.
next_10_search_queries.exclude_if: The user need depends on cloud sync or collaboration.

next_10_search_queries.query: site:chromewebstore.google.com developer utility chrome extension local-only review
next_10_search_queries.hypothesis: Small developer/operator tools can be differentiated by strict local-only behavior.
next_10_search_queries.expected_user_pain: Too much setup for a repeatable browser-side task.
next_10_search_queries.risk: Could fail testability if the flow is tied to complex app state.
next_10_search_queries.why_now: Need discovery surface area beyond the current three archetypes while staying safe.
next_10_search_queries.exclude_if: The happy path cannot be validated in controlled fixtures.
